dtcs.description: Passenger's Door Lock Switch Signal Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs using the HDS.
Cycle the ignition switch to OFF and then back ON.
Operate the passenger's door lock switch in the LOCK and UNLOCK positions for 2 seconds.
Check for DTCs using the HDS. Is DTC B1026 indicated?
steps.no: Intermittent failure. The passenger's door lock system is OK at this time. Check pinfits and connections.

steps.instruction: With the door lock in UNLOCK position, select SECURITY from the HDS and enter the DATA LIST.
Check the ON/OFF information of the Front Passenger's Door Lock Sw. (LOCK) and Front Psnger's Door Lock Sw. (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Are both information indicators OFF?
steps.no: Go to step 9.

steps.instruction: Make sure the ON/OFF information of the Front Psnger's Door Lock Sw. (LOCK) and Front Psnger's Door Lock Sw. (UNLOCK) are changed when the passenger's door lock switch is in the LOCK and UNLOCK positions. Does the information show the correct ON and OFF indications when the door lock switch in the LOCK and UNLOCK positions?
steps.no: Go to step 8.

steps.instruction: Check for a short between the door lock switch LOCK and UNLOCK wires. Are the wire harnesses OK?
steps.yes: Faulty passenger's door lock switch; replace
steps.no: A short in the wire, repair and recheck.

steps.instruction: Remove the passenger's inner handle.
Disconnect the 3P connector from the passenger's door lock switch.
Check the ON/OFF information of the Front Psnger's Door Lock Sw. (LOCK) and Front Psnger's Door Lock Sw. (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Are both information indicators OFF?
steps.yes: Faulty passenger's door lock switch; replace it
steps.no: Go to step 12.

steps.instruction: Check for a short in the wire between the MICU and passenger's door lock switch at the security alarm/keyless door lock system control unit input test. Are the wire harnesses OK?
steps.no: A short in the wire, repair and recheck.

dtcs.description: Trunk Key Cylinder Switch Signal Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs using the HDS.
Cycle the ignition switch to OFF and then back ON.
Operate the trunk key cylinder switch for 2 seconds.
Check the DTCs using the HDS. Is DTC B1027 indicated?
steps.no: Intermittent failure. The trunk key cylinder system is OK at this time.

steps.instruction: With the trunk key cylinder switch in the off (neutral) position, select the TEST MODE MENU from the HDS and enter the DATA LIST.
Check the ON/OFF information of the Trunk Key Cylinder Switch (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Is the information indicator OFF?
steps.no: Go to step 9.

steps.instruction: Make sure the ON/OFF information of the Trunk Key Cylinder Switch (UNLOCK) are changed when the trunk key cylinder switch is turned to UNLOCK position with the key. Does the information show ON when the trunk key cylinder switch is turned to UNLOCK position, and show OFF when the trunk key cylinder switch is returned to the off (neutral) position?
steps.no: Go to step 8.

steps.instruction: Check for an open in the wire between the MICU and trunk key cylinder switch. Are the wire harnesses OK?
steps.yes: Faulty trunk key cylinder switch; replace it.
steps.no: An open in the wire, repair and recheck.

steps.instruction: Disconnect the 3P connector from the trunk key cylinder switch.
Check the ON/OFF information of the Trunk Key Cylinder Switch (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Is the information indicator OFF?
steps.yes: Faulty trunk key cylinder switch; replace it.
steps.no: Go to step 11.

steps.instruction: Check for a short in the wire between the MICU and trunk key cylinder switch. Are the wire harnesses OK?
steps.no: A short in the wire, repair and recheck.

dtcs.description: Headlight Signal Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs using the HDS.
Turn the headlights switch from the OFF position to the ON position.
Check for DTCs using the HDS. Is DTC B1075 indicated?
steps.no: Intermittent failure, the headlight circuit is OK at this time. Check pinfits and connections.

steps.instruction: With the headlight switch OFF, select LIGHTING SYSTEM from the HDS, and enter the DATA LIST.
Check the ON/OFF information of the Headlight Switch (BACK-UP) in the DATA LIST. Is the information indicator OFF?
steps.no: Go to step 10.

steps.instruction: Turn the headlight switch ON.
Check the ON/OFF information of the Headlight Switch (BACK-UP) in the DATA LIST. Is the information indicator ON?
steps.no: Go to step 8.

steps.instruction: Disconnect the relay control module 10P connector (under-hood fuse/relay box connector I) and the wiper/washer switch 8P connector.

Check for continuity between the No. 6 terminal of the relay control module 10P connector and the No. 1 terminal of the wiper/washer switch (combination switch control unit) 8P connector. Is there continuity?
steps.yes: Faulty combination switch control unit; replace the wiper/washer switch.
steps.no: Repair an open in the WHT wire.

steps.instruction: Disconnect the relay control module 10P connector.
Check for continuity between the No. 6 terminal of the relay control module 10P connector I and body ground. Is there continuity?
steps.yes: Repair a short in the WHT wire.
steps.no: Faulty combination switch control unit; replace the wiper/washer switch.

dtcs.description: Windshield Wiper Signal Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs using the HDS.
Cycle the ignition switch to OFF and then back ON (II).
Turn the wiper switch to INT, LOW, then HIGH for at least 2 seconds each.
Check for DTCs using the HDS. Is DTC B1076 indicated?
steps.no: Intermittent failure. The windshield wiper system is OK at this time. Check pinfits and connections.

steps.instruction: With the wiper/washer switch OFF, select WINDSHIELD WIPERS from the HDS, and enter the DATA LIST.
Check the ON/OFF information of the Windshield Wiper Switch (BACK-UP) in the DATA LIST. Is the information indicator OFF?
steps.no: Go to step 11.

steps.instruction: Turn the wiper switch ON (low or high).
Check the ON/OFF information of the Windshield Wiper Switch (BACK-UP) in the DATA LIST. Is the information indicator ON?
steps.yes: Faulty relay control module; replace the under-hood fuse/relay box.
steps.no: Go to step 9.

steps.instruction: Disconnect the relay control module 10P connector and the wiper/washer switch 8P connector.

Check for continuity between the No. 5 terminal of the relay control module 10P connector and the No. 5 terminal of the wiper/washer switch (combination switch control unit) 8P connector. Is there continuity?
steps.yes: Faulty combination switch control unit; replace the wiper/washer switch.
steps.no: Repair an open in the GRN/WHT wire.

steps.instruction: Disconnect the relay control module 10P connector and the wiper/washer switch 8P connector.
Check for continuity between the No. 5 terminal of the relay control module 10P connector and body ground. Is there continuity?
steps.yes: Repair a short in the GRN/WHT wire.
steps.no: Faulty relay control module; replace the under-hood fuse/relay box.

dtcs.description: Windshield Wiper (As) Signal Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs using the HDS.
Cycle the ignition switch to OFF and then back ON (II).
Turn the wiper switch to INT, LOW, then HIGH for at least 2 seconds each.
Check for DTCs using the HDS. Is DTC B1077 indicated?
steps.no: Intermittent failure. The windshield wiper system is OK at this time. Check pinfits and connections.

steps.instruction: Check the No. 7 (10 A), No. 11 (30 A) and No. 21 (7.5 A) fuses in the under-dash fuse/relay box. Are the fuses OK?
steps.no: Replace the blown fuse and recheck the system.

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch ON (II).
Select FUNCTIONAL TEST from the TEST MODE MENU, and operate the windshield wiper motor with FR WIPER (LOW) and FR WIPER (HIGH). Does the windshield wiper motor run?
steps.no: Go to step 15.

steps.instruction: Turn the windshield wiper switch OFF, and stop the wiper arms at the park position. (If the wiper arms do not stop at the park position, turn the ignition switch OFF and stop them at the park position correctly.)
Turn the ignition switch ON (II). Do the wipers begin to run as soon as the ignition switch is cycled ON?
steps.no: Go to step 10.

steps.instruction: Check the ON/OFF information of the Windshield Wiper Motor PARK Switch in the DATA LIST. Is the information indicator OFF?
steps.no: Faulty relay control module; replace the under-hood fuse/relay box.

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch ON (II), and turn the windshield wiper switch ON at low speed.
Check the ON/OFF information of the Windshield Wiper Motor PARK Switch in the DATA LIST. Does the DATA LIST information indicate ON and OFF alternately as the wipers pass the PARK position?
steps.yes: Intermittent failure, the windshield wiper system is OK at this time. Check pinfits and connections.
steps.no: Go to step 13.

steps.instruction: Check for continuity between the No. 4 terminal of the windshield wiper motor 5P connector and No. 5 terminal of the under-hood fuse/relay box 5P connector J. Is there continuity ?
steps.yes: Faulty windshield wiper motor; replace it.
steps.no: Repair an open in the WHT wire.

steps.instruction: Check for continuity between the No. 5 terminal of the under-hood fuse/relay box 5P connector J and body ground. Is there continuity?
steps.yes: Repair a short in the WHT wire.
steps.no: Faulty windshield wiper motor; replace it.

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch OFF, and check the No. 11 (30 A) fuse in the under-dash fuse/relay box. Is the fuse OK?
steps.no: Replace the blown fuse and recheck the system.

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch ON (II).
Check for voltage between the No. 2 terminal of the windshield wiper motor 5P connector and body ground. Is there battery voltage?
steps.no: Repair an open in the GRN wire.

steps.instruction: Check for continuity between the No. 3 and No. 5 terminals of the windshield wiper motor 5P connector and the No. 2 and No. 4 terminal of the under-hood fuse/relay box 5P connector J. Is there continuity?
steps.no: Repair an open in the BLU or YEL wire.

steps.instruction: Perform the wiper motor test.  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Wiper Motor/Testing and Inspection Does the wiper motor operate correctly?
steps.yes: Faulty relay module; Replace the under-hood fuse/relay box.
steps.no: Replace the windshield wiper motor.

steps.no: Go to step 9.

steps.no: Replace the blown bulb and recheck the system.

steps.instruction: Check for continuity between the No. 2 terminal of the headlight (high beam) 2P connectors and the No. 8 (left headlight) or No. 10 (right headlight) terminals of the under-hood fuse/relay box 14P connector H. Is there continuity?
